2604.06086.title: LAG-XAI: A Lie-Inspired Affine Geometric Framework for Interpretable Paraphrasing in Transformer Latent Spaces
2604.06086.paper_summary: Modern Transformer-based language models achieve strong performance in natural language processing tasks, yet their latent semantic spaces remain largely uninterpretable black boxes. This paper introduces LAG-XAI (Lie Affine Geometry for Explainable AI), a novel geometric framework that models paraphrasing not as discrete word substitutions, but as a structured affine transformation within the embedding space. By conceptualizing paraphrasing as a continuous geometric flow on a semantic manifold, we propose a computationally efficient mean-field approximation, inspired by local Lie group actions. This allows us to decompose paraphrase transitions into geometrically interpretable components: rotation, deformation, and translation. Experiments on the noisy PIT-2015 Twitter corpus, encoded with Sentence-BERT, reveal a "linear transparency" phenomenon. The proposed affine operator achieves an AUC of 0.7713. By normalizing against random chance (AUC 0.5), the model captures approximately 80% of the non-linear baseline's effective classification capacity (AUC 0.8405), offering explicit parametric interpretability in exchange for a marginal drop in absolute accuracy. The model identifies fundamental geometric invariants, including a stable matrix reconfiguration angle (~27.84°) and near-zero deformation, indicating local isometry. Cross-domain generalization is confirmed via direct cross-corpus validation on an independent TURL dataset. Furthermore, the practical utility of LAG-XAI is demonstrated in LLM hallucination detection: using a "cheap geometric check," the model automatically detected 95.3% of factual distortions on the HaluEval dataset by registering deviations beyond the permissible semantic corridor. This approach provides a mathematically grounded, resource-efficient path toward the mechanistic interpretability of Transformers.
2604.06086.paper_summary_zh: 現代基於Transformer的語言模型在自然語言處理任務中表現優異，但它們的潛在語義空間仍然大多是不可解釋的黑箱。本文介紹了LAG-XAI（可解釋人工智慧的李仿射幾何），這是一個新穎的幾何框架，將意義重述建模為嵌入空間中的結構性仿射變換，而非離散的單詞替換。通過將意義重述概念化為語義流形上的連續幾何流，我們提出了一種計算上高效的均場近似，靈感來自於局部李群作用。這使我們能夠將意義重述的轉變分解為幾何可解釋的組件：旋轉、變形和位移。在用Sentence-BERT編碼的噪聲PIT-2015 Twitter語料庫上的實驗顯示出一種「線性透明性」現象。所提出的仿射運算符達到了0.7713的AUC。通過與隨機機會（AUC 0.5）進行標準化，該模型捕捉了約80%的非線性基線的有效分類能力（AUC 0.8405），以明確的參數可解釋性換取了絕對準確性的輕微下降。該模型識別出基本的幾何不變量，包括穩定的矩陣重配置角度（約27.84°）和接近零的變形，顯示出局部等距性。通過在獨立的TURL數據集上進行直接的跨語料庫驗證，確認了跨領域的泛化。此外，LAG-XAI在LLM幻覺檢測中的實際效用得到了證明：通過使用「廉價的幾何檢查」，該模型自動檢測了HaluEval數據集上95.3%的事實扭曲，通過註冊超出允許語義範圍的偏差。這種方法提供了一條數學上有根據、資源高效的路徑，朝向Transformer的機械可解釋性。
2604.06086.author: Olexander Mazurets et.al.
2604.06086.authors: Olexander Mazurets, Olexander Barmak, Leonid Bedratyuk, Iurii Krak
